title: Why Users Go There: World Knowledge-Augmented Generative Next POI Recommendation
summary: Generative point-of-interest (POI) recommendation models based on large language models (LLMs) have shown promising results by formulating next POI prediction as a sequence generation task. However, the knowledge encoded in these models remains fixed after training, making them unable to perceive evolving real-world conditions that shape user mobility decisions, such as local events and cultural trends. To bridge this gap, we propose AWARE (Agent-based World knowledge Augmented REcommendation), which employs an LLM agent to generate location- and time-aware contextual narratives that capture regional cultural characteristics, seasonal trends, and ongoing events relevant to each user. Rather than introducing generic or noisy information, AWARE further anchors these narratives in each user's behavioral context, grounding external world knowledge in personalized spatial-temporal patterns. Extensive experiments on three real-world datasets demonstrate that AWARE consistently outperforms competitive baselines, achieving up to 12.4% relative improvement.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种世界知识增强的大语言模型生成式下一个兴趣点（POI）推荐方法AWARE，解决现有LLM-based生成式POI推荐无法感知动态变化的真实世界出行影响因素的问题，在三个真实数据集上取得了相比基线最高12.4%的相对提升。
AI.motivation: 现有基于大语言模型的生成式POI推荐模型，训练完成后编码的知识是固定的，无法感知影响用户出行决策的动态变化真实世界条件，比如本地活动、文化趋势这类信息，存在信息缺陷，需要引入动态的外部世界知识增强推荐效果。
AI.method: 提出AWARE（基于智能体的世界知识增强推荐框架）：利用LLM智能体生成和位置、时间感知的上下文描述，捕捉区域文化特征、季节趋势、和用户相关的当前事件；进一步将这些描述锚定到用户自身行为语境中，把外部世界知识和个性化时空行为模式结合，避免引入通用或噪声信息。
AI.result: 在三个真实世界数据集上进行的大量实验表明，AWARE consistently优于多个有竞争力的基线模型，取得了最高12.4%的相对性能提升。
AI.conclusion: 引入位置时间感知的动态世界知识并将其锚定到用户个性化行为语境，可以有效提升生成式下一个POI推荐的性能，AWARE方法相比现有基线模型获得了显著的效果提升。
AI.author_affiliations: 无法从给定的标题和摘要中确定作者的所属机构信息。
AI.production_deployment: 摘要中未提及真实生产部署、在线A/B测试或工业落地，仅报告了在三个真实世界公开数据集上的离线实验结果。
AI.generative_recommendation: yes，该工作核心就是生成式下一个POI推荐，以基于大语言模型的生成式框架为核心，将下一个POI预测建模为序列生成任务，在问题定义和模型架构上都是以生成式推荐为核心，引入世界知识增强生成式推荐的效果。

title: Intermediate Artifacts as First-Class Citizens: A Data Model for Durable Intermediate Artifacts in Agentic Systems
summary:
Many AI systems are organized around loops in which models reason, call tools, observe results, and continue until a task is complete. These systems often produce final artifacts such as memos, plans, recommendations, and analyses, while the intermediate work that shaped those outputs remains ephemeral. For multi-step, revisable AI work, final artifacts are often lossy projections over upstream state.
  We argue that such systems should preserve durable, inspectable intermediate artifacts: typed, structured, addressable, versioned, dependency-aware, authoritative, and consumable by downstream computation. These artifacts are not the model's private chain-of-thought. They are maintained work products such as evidence maps, claim structures, criteria, assumptions, plans, transformation rules, synthesis procedures, unresolved tensions, and partial products that later humans and agents can inspect, revise, supersede, and improve.
  The contribution is a systems-level data model. We distinguish intermediate artifacts from chat transcripts, memory, hidden chain-of-thought, narration, thinking, and final answers; formalize additive and superseding update semantics with explicit current-state resolution; describe how artifact lineage supports durable intermediate state across revisions; and argue that evaluation must target maintained-state quality, not only final-output quality. The claim is not that artifacts make models smarter. It is that durable intermediate artifacts make AI-generated work more inspectable, revisable, and maintainable over time.

AI.tldr: This paper proposes treating intermediate artifacts in agentic AI systems as first-class citizens, presenting a systems-level data model for durable, inspectable intermediate work products to improve AI work maintainability and revisability.
AI.motivation: Current agentic AI systems typically only retain final outputs and discard intermediate work, which is problematic for multi-step revisable AI tasks because final artifacts are often lossy projections of upstream state, lacking transparency and support for revision over time.
AI.method: The paper argues for preserving structured, typed, versioned, dependency-aware intermediate work products (distinct from private chain-of-thought, chat transcripts or memory), and proposes a formal systems-level data model with additive and superseding update semantics, explicit current-state resolution, and lineage tracking for intermediate artifacts.
AI.result: A complete systems-level data model for durable intermediate artifacts is defined, which clarifies the distinctions between intermediate artifacts and other related concepts (chain-of-thought, chat logs, memory, final outputs), and establishes that evaluation of agentic systems should include maintained-state quality in addition to final output quality.
AI.conclusion: Durable intermediate artifacts, rather than making models smarter, make AI-generated work significantly more inspectable, revisable, and maintainable over time, and should be treated as first-class citizens in agentic system design.
AI.author_affiliations: Cannot be determined from the given abstract.
AI.production_deployment: The abstract is silent on real production deployment, live A/B tests, or industrial adoption.
AI.generative_recommendation: No. This work focuses on a general data model for intermediate artifacts in agentic AI systems, and is not substantively related to generative recommendation for end-to-end item/content recommendation tasks.

title: Checkup2Action: A Multimodal Clinical Check-up Report Dataset for Patient-Oriented Action Card Generation
summary: Clinical check-up reports are multimodal documents that combine page layouts, tables, numerical biomarkers, abnormality flags, imaging findings, and domain-specific terminology. Such heterogeneous evidence is difficult for laypersons to interpret and translate into concrete follow-up actions. Although large language models show promise in medical summarisation and triage support, their ability to generate safe, prioritised, and patient-oriented actions from multimodal check-up reports remains under-benchmarked. We present \textbf{Checkup2Action}, a multimodal clinical check-up report dataset and benchmark for structured \textit{Action Card} generation. Each card describes one clinically relevant issue and specifies its priority, recommended department, follow-up time window, patient-facing explanation, and questions for clinicians, while avoiding diagnostic or treatment-prescriptive claims. The dataset contains 2,000 de-identified real-world check-up reports covering demographic information, physical examinations, laboratory tests, cardiovascular assessments, imaging-related evidence, and physician summaries. We formulate checkup-to-action generation as a constrained structured generation task and introduce an evaluation protocol covering issue coverage and precision, priority consistency, department and time recommendation accuracy, action complexity, usefulness, readability, and safety compliance. Experiments with general-purpose and medical large language models reveal clear trade-offs between issue coverage, action correctness, conciseness, and safety alignment. Checkup2Action provides a new multimodal benchmark for evaluating patient-oriented reasoning over clinical check-up reports.

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了一个名为Checkup2Action的多模态体检报告数据集与基准，用于面向患者的结构化行动卡片生成，提出了任务形式和评估协议，并通过实验对比不同大语言模型得到了相关结论，为该方向研究提供了评测基础。
AI.motivation: 普通民众难以解读体检报告中多模态异质信息并转化为具体后续行动，现有大语言模型在从多模态体检报告生成安全、优先级明确、面向患者的行动方面仍缺乏充分的基准评测。
AI.method: 构建包含2000份去标识化真实世界体检报告的Checkup2Action数据集，每个样本对应结构化行动卡片，包含临床问题、优先级、建议科室、随访时间窗、患者易懂解释和给临床医生的问题；将体检报告转行动生成建模为约束结构化生成任务，设计了覆盖问题覆盖率、准确率、优先级一致性、科室时间推荐准确率、行动复杂度、实用性、可读性和安全性合规性的评估协议，在通用和医学大语言模型上开展实验。
AI.result: 实验发现不同大语言模型在问题覆盖率、行动正确性、简洁性和安全对齐之间存在明显的权衡关系。
AI.conclusion: Checkup2Action为评估基于临床体检报告的面向患者推理提供了一个新的多模态基准，填补了该领域的 benchmark 空白。
AI.author_affiliations: 无法从摘要中确定作者的具体机构 affiliations，仅能知道作者团队包含来自不同研究机构的研究者，其中Philip Torr通常与牛津大学相关，但摘要未明确说明，因此无法确定所有作者 affiliations。
AI.production_deployment: 摘要未提及该工作是否进行了真实生产部署、线上A/B测试或产业应用，仅说明提供了数据集和基准，工作停留在数据集构建和离线实验阶段。
AI.generative_recommendation: no。该工作研究的是面向患者的体检报告行动卡片生成任务，属于医疗临床自然语言生成领域，与面向用户/物品的推荐任务无关，不涉及生成式推荐的内容。

title: TwiSTAR:Think Fast, Think Slow, Then Act,Generative Recommendation with Adaptive Reasoning
summary: Generative recommendation with Semantic IDs (SIDs) has emerged as a promising paradigm, yet existing methods apply a fixed inference strategy, either fast direct generation or slow chain-of-thought reasoning, uniformly across all user histories. This approach creates a trade-off: fast recommendation model produces suboptimal accuracy on hard samples, while always invoking slow reasoning incurs prohibitive latency and wastes computation on easy cases. To address this, we propose Think Fast, Think Slow, Then Act, a framework that learns to adaptively allocate reasoning effort per user sequence. Our system equips an LLM with three complementary tools: a fast SID-based retriever, a lightweight candidate ranker, and a slow reasoning model that generates explicit rationales before recommending. Crucially, we inject collaborative commonsense into the slow model by transforming item-to-item knowledge into natural language explanations. A planner, trained through supervised warm-up followed by agentic reinforcement learning, dynamically decides which tool to invoke. Experiments on three datasets demonstrate that our method outperforms strong baselines, achieving consistent accuracy gains while reducing inference latency compared to uniform slow reasoning.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了名为TwiSTAR的自适应推理生成推荐框架，针对基于语义ID的生成推荐固定推理策略存在的精度-延迟权衡问题，通过 planner 动态分配不同速度的推理工具，在提升推荐精度的同时降低推理延迟。
AI.motivation: 现有基于语义ID的生成推荐方法对所有用户历史统一采用固定推理策略：快速直接生成在难样本上精度不佳，始终使用慢的思维链推理会导致延迟过高、在简单样本上浪费计算资源，存在难以兼顾精度和效率的权衡问题。
AI.method: 提出TwiSTAR（Think Fast, Think Slow, Then Act）框架，学习为每个用户序列自适应分配推理工作量：为大语言模型配备三个互补工具：基于语义ID的快速检索器、轻量级候选排序器、生成显式理由后再推荐的慢推理模型；将物品间知识转换为自然语言解释，把协作常识注入慢推理模型；训练一个planner，先经监督预热再用智能体强化学习训练，动态决定调用哪个工具。
AI.result: 在三个公开数据集上的实验表明，该方法优于强基线模型，相比统一慢推理，在获得 consistent 精度提升的同时降低了推理延迟。
AI.conclusion: 自适应推理策略能够有效解决生成推荐中精度和推理延迟的权衡问题，TwiSTAR框架在保持推荐精度提升的同时有效降低了整体推理延迟，优于现有固定推理策略的生成推荐方法。
AI.author_affiliations: 无法从给定标题和摘要中推断出作者的 affiliations
AI.production_deployment: 摘要中未提及真实生产部署、线上A/B测试或工业落地，仅报告了在公开三个数据集上的离线实验结果。
AI.generative_recommendation: yes，该工作核心就是生成式推荐研究，针对基于语义ID的生成推荐范式，提出了带自适应推理的生成推荐框架，慢推理模块会先生成显式理由再生成推荐结果，属于端到端生成式推荐，问题建模和模型架构都围绕生成式推荐展开。

title: Conditional Memory Enhanced Item Representation for Generative Recommendation
summary: Generative recommendation (GR) has emerged as a promising paradigm that predicts target items by autoregressively generating their semantic identifiers (SID). Most GR methods follow a quantization-representation-generation pipeline, first assigning each item a SID, then constructing input representations from SID-token embeddings, and finally predicting the target SID through autoregressive generation. Existing item-level representation constructions mainly take two forms: directly merging SID-token embeddings into a compact vector, or enriching item-level representations with external inputs through additional networks. However, these item-level constructors still expose two practical challenges: direct merging may amplify the information loss caused by quantization and ID collision while obscuring SID code relations, whereas external-input-based methods can strengthen item semantics but cannot reliably preserve the SID-structured evidence required for token-level generation. These limitations make representation construction an underexplored bottleneck, leading to two severe problems, \ie{} the Identity-Structure Preservation Conflict and Input-Output Granularity Mismatch. To this end, we propose ComeIR, a Conditional Memory enhanced Item Representation framework that reconstructs SID-token embeddings into item-aware inputs and restores the token granularity during SID decoding. Specifically, MM-guided token scoring adaptively estimates the contribution of each code within the SID, dual-level Engram memory captures intra-item code composition and inter-item transition patterns, and a memory-restoring prediction head reuses the memories during SID decoding. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness and flexibility of ComeIR, and further reveal scalable gains from enlarging conditional memory.

AI.tldr: 本文针对生成式推荐中当前项目表示构建存在的问题，提出了条件记忆增强的项目表示框架ComeIR，通过自适应评分、双级记忆存储和记忆复用恢复 token 粒度，实验证明了该方法的有效性和可扩展性。
AI.motivation: 现有生成式推荐的项目表示构建方法存在明显缺陷：直接合并SID token嵌入会放大量化和ID碰撞带来的信息损失，还会模糊SID代码关系；基于外部输入的方法虽然能增强项目语义，但无法可靠保留token级生成所需的SID结构化证据，进而导致了身份-结构保留冲突和输入-输出粒度不匹配两大问题，成为生成式推荐的性能瓶颈，需要解决。
AI.method: 提出条件记忆增强的项目表示框架ComeIR，具体包括三个核心部分：1）MM引导的token评分，自适应估计SID中每个代码的贡献；2）双级Engram记忆，捕捉项目内代码组成和项目间转移模式；3）记忆恢复预测头，在SID解码过程中复用存储的记忆，将SID token嵌入重建为项目感知输入，在解码时恢复token粒度。
AI.result: 大量实验结果表明ComeIR具备有效性和灵活性，并且实验进一步发现增大条件记忆可以带来可扩展的性能增益。
AI.conclusion: 所提出的ComeIR框架能够有效解决生成式推荐中项目表示构建的现有瓶颈，提升生成式推荐性能，且该方法具备可扩展性，增大条件记忆可进一步提升效果。
AI.author_affiliations: 无法从给定的标题和摘要中确定作者的 affiliations。
AI.production_deployment: 摘要中未提及真实生产部署、线上A/B测试或工业落地，仅说明进行了离线扩展实验。
AI.generative_recommendation: yes，该工作核心就是针对生成式推荐的项目表示构建问题进行研究，问题面向生成式推荐的自回归生成范式，模型设计围绕生成式推荐的SID生成流程优化，属于生成式推荐领域的核心改进工作。

title: Quality-Aware Collaborative Multi-Positive Contrastive Learning for Sequential Recommendation
summary: The effectiveness of contrastive learning in sequential recommendation hinges on the construction of contrastive views, which ideally should be both semantically consistent and diverse. However, most existing CL-based methods rely on heuristic augmentations that are prone to removing crucial items or disrupting transition patterns, leading to semantic drift. While a few studies have explored learnable augmentations to improve view quality, they often suffer from limited diversity and still necessitate heuristic aids. Furthermore, the quality differences across views are rarely modeled explicitly and adaptively, aggravating the false-positive issue. To address these issues, we propose Quality-aware Collaborative Multi-Positive Contrastive Learning for sequential recommendation. First, we introduce a learnable collaborative sequence augmentation module that generates two augmented views under two complementary collaborative contexts, one based on same-target sequences and the other on similar sequences, thereby enhancing view diversity while preserving intent consistency.Second, we design a quality-aware mechanism, tightly integrated into the model representations, which estimates each view' s quality from the confidence of its augmentation operations and assigns adaptive weights to ensure that high-confidence views contribute more supervision while low-confidence ones contribute less.Extensive experiments on three real-world datasets demonstrate that QCMP-CL outperforms state-of-the-art CL-based sequential recommendation baselines.

AI.tldr: 本文针对对比学习在序列推荐中存在的视图构造缺陷（语义漂移、多样性不足、未显式建模视图质量差异导致的假阳性问题），提出了一种质量感知的协同多正例对比学习方法QCMP-CL，通过可学习协同序列增强和质量感知自适应加权机制提升模型性能，实验验证其在多个真实数据集上优于现有基于对比学习的最优序列推荐基线模型。
AI.motivation: 对比学习在序列推荐中的效果依赖于兼具语义一致性和多样性的对比视图构造，现有方法存在以下缺陷：大多数基于对比学习的方法依赖启发式增强，容易丢失关键物品、破坏转移模式，导致语义漂移；少数可学习增强方法存在多样性不足的问题，仍需要启发式辅助，且很少显式自适应建模不同视图间的质量差异，加剧了假阳性问题。
AI.method: 1. 提出可学习的协同序列增强模块，基于同目标序列和相似序列两种互补协同上下文生成两个增强视图，在保留意图一致性的同时提升视图多样性；2. 设计与模型表示紧密集成的质量感知机制，根据增强操作的置信度估计每个视图的质量，分配自适应权重，让高置信度视图提供更多监督，低置信度视图提供更少监督。
AI.result: 在三个真实世界数据集上进行的大量实验表明，所提出的QCMP-CL模型性能优于所有现有基于对比学习的最优序列推荐基线模型。
AI.conclusion: 针对序列推荐中对比学习视图构造的现有缺陷，所提出的质量感知协同多正例对比学习框架能够有效提升视图质量，缓解语义漂移和假阳性问题，取得了优于SOTA基线的性能。
AI.author_affiliations: 无法从给定的标题和摘要中推断作者的 affiliations。
AI.production_deployment: 摘要中未提及真实生产部署、线上A/B测试或工业落地相关内容，仅在公开真实数据集上进行了离线实验。
AI.generative_recommendation: no，本文核心是改进对比学习的视图构造与加权机制，用于序列推荐的表示学习，不属于端到端生成式推荐，工作核心并非用生成模型直接生成推荐物品或内容。

title: FedMM: Federated Collaborative Signal Quantization for Multi-Market CTR Prediction
summary: Online platforms such as Amazon and Netflix serve users across multiple countries and regions, underscoring the importance of multi-market recommendation (MMR). Most MMR methods adopt a pre-training and fine-tuning paradigm, in which a unified model is first trained on centralized, global data and subsequently adapted to specific markets. However, this approach ignores the privacy of market data. While traditional federated learning preserves privacy, it typically aims to obtain a global model by aggregating model parameters and does not account for significant market heterogeneity. Additionally, because ID spaces are disjoint across markets, embedding-based aggregation strategies become ineffective. To overcome these challenges, we propose a federated collaborative signal quantization (FedMM) method for multi-market click-through rate (CTR) prediction. Our core idea leverages a discrete codebook mechanism to achieve privacy-preserving transmission and align disjoint ID spaces. We further employ a hierarchical codebook structure to capture cross-market shared patterns and market-specific characteristics. Specifically, we deploy a residual quantized variational autoencoder (RQ-VAE) with a dual-layer codebook mechanism for each market to quantize collaborative embeddings. The first layer utilizes a global federated codebook, updated via aggregation to capture universally shared collaborative patterns, while the second layer maintains a local codebook to learn market-specific semantics. Finally, the learned discrete codes, which integrate both general and specific collaborative signals, are incorporated into downstream CTR models to enhance prediction accuracy across all markets. Extensive experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrate that FedMM significantly improves recommendation performance with privacy guarantees.

AI.tldr: 本文针对多市场点击率预测中现有方法存在隐私泄露、无法处理市场异质性和跨市场ID空间不相交的问题，提出了联邦协作信号量化方法FedMM，在保证隐私的前提下提升了多市场CTR预测性能。
AI.motivation: 现有多市场推荐大多采用集中式全局数据预训练加微调范式，会忽略市场数据隐私；传统联邦学习聚焦聚合参数得到全局模型，既没有考虑显著的市场异质性，且由于不同市场ID空间不相交，基于嵌入的聚合策略也无法生效，因此需要新的方法解决这些挑战。
AI.method: 提出FedMM方法，核心是利用离散码本机制实现隐私保护传输并对齐不相交的ID空间，采用分层码本结构捕捉跨市场共享模式和市场特定特征：每个市场部署带双层码本机制的残差量化变分自编码器（RQ-VAE）对协作嵌入进行量化，第一层是通过聚合更新的全局联邦码本捕捉通用共享协作模式，第二层维护本地码本学习市场特定语义，融合通用和特定协作信号的离散编码后输入下游CTR模型提升预测效果。
AI.result: 在基准数据集上的大量实验表明，FedMM在保证隐私的前提下，显著提升了多市场推荐（CTR预测）的性能。
AI.conclusion: FedMM可以有效解决多市场CTR预测中隐私保护、市场异质性和ID空间不相交的问题，在隐私保障下显著提升预测性能。
AI.author_affiliations: 无法从给定摘要中确定作者的 affiliations。
AI.production_deployment: 摘要仅提到在基准数据集上进行实验，未提及真实生产部署、在线A/B测试或工业落地，因此未说明是否有实际生产部署。
AI.generative_recommendation: no，本文研究的是面向多市场CTR预测的联邦学习方法，核心是协作信号量化与分层码本设计，并非以端到端生成式推荐为核心，不涉及生成式推荐的核心流程。

title: AgentDisCo: Towards Disentanglement and Collaboration in Open-ended Deep Research Agents
summary: In this paper, we present AgentDisCo, a novel Disentangled and Collaborative agentic architecture that formulates deep research as an adversarial optimization problem between information exploration and exploitation. Unlike existing approaches that conflate these two processes into a single module, AgentDisCo employs a critic agent to evaluate generated outlines and refine search queries, and a generator agent to retrieve updated results and revise outlines accordingly. The iteratively refined outline is then passed to a downstream report writer that synthesizes a comprehensive research report. The overall workflow supports both handcrafted and automatically discovered design strategies via a meta-optimization harness, in which the generator agent is repurposed as a scoring agent to evaluate critic outputs and generate quality signals. Powerful code-generation agents (e.g., Claude-Code, Codex) systematically explore agent configurations and construct a policy bank, a structured repository of reusable design strategies, enabling the framework to self-refine without extensive human intervention. We evaluate AgentDisCo on three established deep research benchmarks (DeepResearchBench, DeepConsult, DeepResearchGym) using Gemini-2.5-Pro, achieving performance comparable to or surpassing leading closed-source systems. Observing that existing benchmarks inadequately reflect real-world user needs, we introduce GALA (General AI Life Assistants), a benchmark that mines latent research interests from users' historical browsing behavior. We further develop a rendering agent that converts research reports into visually rich poster presentations, and demonstrate an end-to-end product, AutoResearch Your Interest, which delivers personalized deep research recommendations derived from individual browsing histories.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了用于开放式深度研究智能体的解耦协作架构AgentDisCo，在现有基准上取得了媲美领先闭源系统的性能，同时提出了新基准GALA，并展示了端到端个性化深度研究产品。
AI.motivation: 现有深度研究智能体方法将信息探索和利用过程合并到单个模块中，且现有基准无法充分反映真实世界用户需求，因此需要设计更合理的智能体架构并构建更贴合实际的评估基准。
AI.method: AgentDisCo将深度研究建模为信息探索和利用之间的对抗优化问题，解耦为两个智能体：critic智能体评估生成的大纲并优化搜索查询，generator智能体检索结果并修订大纲，迭代后生成的大纲交付给下游报告编写器生成研究报告；通过元优化框架支持人工设计和自动发现的设计策略，利用代码生成智能体探索智能体配置构建可复用策略库，实现无需大量人工干预的自优化；此外构建了从用户历史浏览行为挖掘潜在研究兴趣的新基准GALA，还开发了渲染智能体将研究报告转换为可视化海报。
AI.result: 在三个已有的深度研究基准上使用Gemini-2.5-Pro评估，AgentDisCo性能可媲美或超过领先的闭源系统；提出的新基准GALA更贴合真实用户需求，所展示的端到端产品AutoResearch Your Interest可基于用户浏览历史提供个性化深度研究推荐。
AI.conclusion: 解耦协作的架构AgentDisCo能够有效提升开放式深度研究智能体的性能，所提出的新基准GALA可以更好反映真实用户需求，该框架可以通过自优化不断改进，能够支撑端到端个性化深度研究产品。
AI.author_affiliations: 无法从给定摘要中确定作者的机构和 affiliations。
AI.production_deployment: 摘要展示了端到端产品AutoResearch Your Interest，但未说明是否进行了实际生产部署、在线A/B测试或工业采用，仅在公共基准上进行了实验。
AI.generative_recommendation: no，该工作核心是面向深度研究任务的多智能体架构，研究内容是深度研究报告生成，并非以端到端生成推荐项目/内容为核心的推荐任务，与生成式推荐没有实质性关联。
